title: Adapt existing doctor checks to v2.0 layout (no new lint checks)
description: Update the 4 v1.x-aware doctor checks (taxonomy_missing, rules_dir_unindexed, stable_id_collision, bootstrap_missing) so they no longer false-positive on the v2.0 layout. taxonomy_missing → renamed/replaced with knowledge_dir_missing (checks .fabric/knowledge/{5 subdirs} + pending/ existence). rules_dir_unindexed → knowledge_dir_unindexed (checks each knowledge subdir for files referenced in agents.meta.json nodes). stable_id_collision → extended to validate counter monotonicity in addition to uniqueness. bootstrap_missing → either deleted (no v2.0 bootstrap dir) or repointed to AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md root pointers. Does NOT add the 6 new lint checks (orphan demote / stale archive / id duplicate / layer mismatch / index drift / pending overdue) — those are explicitly rc.4 scope. The goal is keep doctor green on a fresh `fabric init` v2.0 repo.

files.target: doctor checks at lines 285-305 (the additive list of 19 checks)
files.change: Replace `taxonomy_missing` with `knowledge_dir_missing` — checks .fabric/knowledge/ exists and contains all 5 type subdirs + pending/. Replace `rules_dir_unindexed` with `knowledge_dir_unindexed` — iterates new layout. Extend `stable_id_collision` (line 277): in addition to uniqueness, verify all KP-/KT- ids in agents.meta.json have counter ≤ counters.{KP|KT} envelope value (catches manually edited meta files). Delete or rename `bootstrap_missing`: if AGENTS.md exists at repo root, mark check passed; else fixable_error with hint to run fabric init. Add inspection for `legacy_v1_artifacts_present` (warn-level): scans for residual `.fabric/rules/`, `.fabric/INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md`, `.fabric/bootstrap/` and reports stale leftovers (helpful for manual upgrades, even though TASK-001 handles fresh delete in self-repo).

When --fix runs, ensure recreation logic uses TASK-008's v2.0 layout: missing knowledge subdirs are recreated via fs.mkdir; missing agents.meta.json is rebuilt with empty counters {KP:0, KT:0}; events.jsonl creation unchanged. Do NOT regenerate INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md (deleted in TASK-001). Do NOT recreate .fabric/bootstrap/ (gone in v2.0).

Extend to detect counter desync: if any node in agents.meta.json has stable_id 'KP-D-7' but counters.KP is 5, flag as fixable_error 'counter_desync' — fix path: bump counters.KP to max(observed_id_for_KP, current). This catches manual edits to meta or interrupted scan runs.

implementation: Identify the 4 v1.x-coupled checks: taxonomy_missing (~line 680), rules_dir_unindexed, stable_id_collision (~line 277), bootstrap_missing
Rename taxonomy_missing → knowledge_dir_missing; rewrite inspection to glob .fabric/knowledge/{decisions,pitfalls,guidelines,models,processes,pending}/
Rename rules_dir_unindexed → knowledge_dir_unindexed; iterate new layout
Extend stable_id_collision to also check counter_desync invariant
Decide bootstrap_missing fate: rename → bootstrap_anchor_missing (checks repo-root AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists) per v2.0 dual-anchor model
Add new check legacy_v1_artifacts_present (warn-only, not fixable in this rc — just visibility)
Update --fix paths to honor new layout (mkdir new subdirs instead of recreating .fabric/rules/)
Run existing doctor.test.ts — adapt fixtures from .fabric/rules/ → .fabric/knowledge/decisions/
Add 4+ new test fixtures: clean v2.0 repo (all checks pass), partial knowledge layout (missing pending/), stable_id collision, counter desync

reference.pattern: doctor.ts additive check list pattern + fixable/manual classification at lines 285-305
reference.examples: Each check follows: `function inspectFoo(ctx): DoctorIssue[]` + `function createFooCheck(): DoctorCheck { return { id: 'foo', inspect: inspectFoo, ... } }`. Add to checks array. Per code_anchors line: '19 checks in additive list pattern' — purely additive surgery, not refactor.

criteria: Running `fabric doctor` on a fresh post-init v2.0 repo (output of TASK-008) reports zero errors and zero warnings
Running `fabric doctor` on a repo with .fabric/knowledge/decisions/ missing flags fixable_error 'knowledge_dir_missing' with clear remediation
Running `fabric doctor --fix` on the above creates the missing subdir + rewrites agents.meta.json appropriately
stable_id_collision detects two nodes with the same id (regression test still passes)
counter_desync detected: meta has KP-D-7 but counters.KP=5 → fixable_error; --fix bumps counters.KP to 7
legacy_v1_artifacts_present fires (warn) on a repo where someone manually re-created .fabric/rules/ post-upgrade
bootstrap_anchor_missing passes when AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md exists at repo root, fails otherwise
doctor --fix does NOT regenerate INITIAL_TAXONOMY.md or .fabric/bootstrap/ (verified by snapshot test)
All existing doctor tests pass after fixture path updates
doctor count increases from 19 → 20 (rename net-zero + 1 new legacy_v1_artifacts_present check)

rationale.chosen_approach: Rename + extend existing checks, add 1 visibility-only legacy detector; defer all 6 lint checks to rc.4
alternatives_considered: Add the 6 lint checks now (rejected — explicit rc.4 scope per handoff)
Delete bootstrap_missing entirely (rejected — repo-root AGENTS.md anchor still has invariants worth checking)
decision_factors: Counter desync is a real risk introduced by TASK-003's writable counter, must be guarded NOW
legacy_v1_artifacts_present is warn-only — no behavioral cost, useful diagnostic
rationale.tradeoffs: Doctor stays at v1.x check coverage levels (19→20); no new safety nets for layer/maturity correctness until rc.4 — acceptable per scope boundary

manual_checks: Run doctor on fresh post-init repo — green
Manually delete one knowledge subdir, run doctor — flagged
Manually edit agents.meta.json to introduce counter desync — flagged + fixable
success_metrics: 100% pass rate on fresh-init v2.0 fixture
All 4 renamed checks have parity coverage with v1.x equivalents
Counter desync detected in 100% of injected fault scenarios
